blockingIssues.description: merge_server() in discovery.rs merges agent_ports from multiple sources but never re-classifies agent_types from the merged port set. After reconciling a server with ports [8390] (Codex) and [9234] (Pi), agent_types will be [PiNative, PiAcp] from the preferred Bonjour source, missing Codex entirely. Fix: after merge_agent_ports(), add `existing.agent_types = classify_agent_ports(&existing.agent_ports);`

VAL-DISC-025 test asserts merged agent_ports but does NOT assert agent_types after merge, so the merge_server bug is not caught. The test should assert agent_types contains all expected types after merge.

Rust reconnect.rs:178 still checks for literal string "directCodex" to identify direct agent connections, but Android SavedServerStore.kt migrates "directCodex" → "direct" on deserialization and iOS DiscoveredServer.swift maps it to .directAgent. If Rust receives the migrated value "direct", resolved_preferred_agent_port returns None and silently drops the user's preferred agent port. Rust should accept both "directCodex" and "direct" (or use the new canonical name).

nonBlockingIssues.description: The "directCodex" connection mode string literal is preserved in reconnect.rs as a persisted platform value. Should be tracked for coordinated rename when platform migration is complete.
Comment at ssh.rs:1043 still calls the renamed steps 'Codex-specific' even though names are now generic. Cosmetic only.
collect_browser_mdns_seeds() only browses _codex._tcp. services, never _agent._tcp. even though classification logic accepts them.
GenericAcp returns None from agent_binary_name(), meaning no binary validation occurs for GenericAcp agents in the provider path. May be intentional (remote_command pattern) but is undocumented.
ManualConnectionMode.codex enum case retains 'codex' name — intentional since it refers to Codex WebSocket protocol specifically, but could be renamed for full consistency.
Test in reconnect.rs uses "directCodex" as preferred_connection_mode but there's no test for the new canonical "direct" mode.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add canonical connection mode string names: specify that the preferred connection mode should be stored as "direct" (not "directCodex" or "directAgent") across all platforms. Rust, iOS, and Android should all agree on the canonical string value. The current split (Rust: "directCodex", Android: "direct", iOS: ".directAgent" enum) causes cross-platform serialization mismatches.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: discovery-milestone-build-gate review found reconnect.rs:178 checks "directCodex" while Android migrates to "direct" and iOS uses enum .directAgent. This is a cross-platform consistency issue.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the SSH method hierarchy: ssh_connect_only() (generic, no agent install) → ssh_connect_and_bootstrap() (Codex-only, includes binary resolution and install) → connect_remote_over_ssh_with_agent_type() (agent-type-aware routing). Also document the is_non_codex_agent routing function and the two-path design (guided vs provider).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: ssh-bootstrap-agent-separation review noted these are key architectural decisions not documented anywhere.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the AGENT_PORT_REGISTRY pattern and the requirement to add entries for new AgentType variants. Future workers adding new agent types need to know this registry exists and must be updated.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: agent-aware-discovery-config review noted the registry is not documented in any library file.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker did not add new tests for rust-discovery-field-rename (pure rename).
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — reviewer noted this is a reasonable deviation since 1250 existing tests exercise all renamed fields
